title: Refactor DIP-004 (DIP): Direct instantiation of VectorPlotter dependency.

detected_issue.reasoning: JointGrid, a high-level layout manager, directly instantiates VectorPlotter to handle variable assignment. This prevents the use of alternative data processing strategies and couples the grid layout logic to a specific data-handling implementation.

fix_logic.summary: Inject VectorPlotter dependency into JointGrid constructor
fix_logic.rationale: By allowing the VectorPlotter class to be passed as an optional argument (defaulting to the existing implementation), we decouple JointGrid from the concrete VectorPlotter class, adhering to the Dependency Inversion Principle.

changes.search_text:

        x=None, y=None, hue=None,
        height=6, ratio=5, space=.2,
        palette=None, hue_order=None, hue_norm=None,
        dropna=False, xlim=None, ylim=None, marginal_ticks=False,
    ):

        # Set up the subplot grid
changes.replace_text:
def __init__(
        self, data=None, *,
        x=None, y=None, hue=None,
        height=6, ratio=5, space=.2,
        palette=None, hue_order=None, hue_norm=None,
        dropna=False, xlim=None, ylim=None, marginal_ticks=False,
        plotter_class=None,
    ):

        # Set up the subplot grid

changes.search_text:
# Process the input variables
        p = VectorPlotter(data=data, variables=dict(x=x, y=y, hue=hue))
        plot_data = p.plot_data.loc[:, p.plot_data.notna().any()]
changes.replace_text:
# Process the input variables
        if plotter_class is None:
            plotter_class = VectorPlotter
        p = plotter_class(data=data, variables=dict(x=x, y=y, hue=hue))
        plot_data = p.plot_data.loc[:, p.plot_data.notna().any()]
